preview_evidence: .ai/runs/2026-07-23T16-50-50.059Z_audio-to-score-form-first-skill/verify.json; rendered UI review is not applicable because no product surface changed

summary.rationale: The non-visual workflow keeps musical form as the primary consumer structure, makes the four-measure system preference conditional on readability, preserves section authority and provenance, and defines clear publication success and failure checks.

notes: The workflow has a single ordered publication path after final-score validation.
Automatic-draft, approval, uncertainty, asset failure, responsive failure, and generated-layer states remain explicit.
The form digest precedes notation, improving whole-song orientation and scanability.
Four-measure systems are a readable default with explicit section, pickup, meter, density, and viewport exceptions.
The consumer checklist covers first, middle, final, repeated, desktop, mobile, assets, and playback.
No rendered styling is introduced; the layout guidance is grounded in score readability and musical structure.
The task is a non-visual skill contract. Its future consumer guidance aligns with the Music Visual Instrument register by making form, measures, sections, and score readability authoritative.
